app_info.subtitle: Read campaign lore
app_info.description: Archivist AI lets users browse their tabletop campaign data in ChatGPT, including campaigns, characters, sessions, story beats, moments, factions, locations, items, quests, journals, journal folders, and entity links.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves the caller's campaign list from Archivist AI and does not modify data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content or change public internet state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

test_cases.description: List available campaigns for the authenticated user. Demo account contains a single seeded campaign; listing may return one result.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a paginated campaign list with at least one campaign, including title, description, system, and pagination fields (total, current_page, per_page).

test_cases.description: Inspect campaign statistics. Demo account has one campaign; the model may call list-campaigns-tool first to resolve it.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns campaign statistics such as character, session, and moment counts for the user's campaign.

test_cases.description: Find approved player characters. Demo account has one campaign; the model may call list-campaigns-tool first to resolve it.
test_cases.user_prompt: List approved player characters in my campaign.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns approved PC character records with names and character metadata. Empty list is acceptable only if the seeded campaign has no approved PCs.

test_cases.description: Retrieve a session with related story context. Demo account is seeded with sessions; the model may list campaigns and sessions first.
test_cases.user_prompt: Open a game session from my campaign and include related beats and moments.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns session details (title, summary, session_date) and includes beats and/or moments when include_beats and include_moments are requested.

test_cases.description: Review in-progress quests. Demo account is seeded with quests; the model may call list-campaigns-tool first to resolve the campaign.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show in-progress quests for my campaign.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns quests filtered to in-progress status with quest names, status, and pagination fields. At least one in-progress quest is expected in the seeded demo account.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for write/create requests. Prompt avoids naming Archivist so routing is not biased toward the connector.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Create a new side quest called The Broken Crown for my D&D campaign.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked. This MCP server is read-only and cannot create or edit quests.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for unrelated calendar requests.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: What meetings do I have tomorrow?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because calendar data is outside its supported workflows.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for general tabletop advice that does not require campaign records.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Give me five tips for running a better combat encounter.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because the request does not require reading the user's campaign data.
